title: Net Salary in Europe 2026: The Gross-to-Net Gap in Italy, Germany, France and Spain
title_hi: Net Salary in Europe 2026: The Gross-to-Net Gap in Italy, Germany, France and Spain
summary: Most European workers are surprised when they see their first payslip. The number on the contract and the number on the
content:
Most European workers are surprised when they see their first payslip. The number on the contract and the number on the payslip rarely match — and the gap is larger than most people expect.

In 2026, the gross-to-net gap varies dramatically across the four largest economies in Europe. Here's what workers actually take home.


  
  
  Italy: IRPEF + INPS


Italy applies a progressive IRPEF (income tax) on top of INPS social contributions (~9.19% employee side). A €30,000 gross salary yields roughly €21,500 net — a 28% deduction.

Calculate your Italian net salary: https://www.vextorcapital.com/it/tools/calcolo-stipendio-netto


  
  
  Germany: Einkommensteuer + Sozialversicherung


Germany has one of Europe's highest total burdens. The Einkommensteuer tariff (§32a EStG 2026), combined with RV (9.3%), KV (~8.75%), PV (1.8–2.4%), and ALV (1.3%), means €30,000 gross → ~€20,600 net.

Calculate your German net salary: https://www.vextorcapital.com/de/tools/brutto-netto-rechner


  
  
  France: IR + Cotisations Sociales


France's cotisations sociales are the highest in Europe (~22% employee side). Income tax (IR) adds on top. €30,000 gross → ~€23,000 net after all deductions.

Calculate your French net salary: https://www.vextorcapital.com/fr/tools/calculateur-brut-net


  
  
  Spain: IRPF + Seguridad Social


Spain applies IRPF (progressive, state + regional average) plus 6.5% Seguridad Social. €30,000 gross → ~€22,800 net.

Calculate your Spanish net salary: https://www.vextorcapital.com/es/tools/calculadora-sueldo-neto


  
  
  Key Takeaway


The country with the smallest gross-to-net gap is Spain (~24%), followed by France (~23%), Italy (~28%), and Germany (~31%) at the €30k income level.

If you're negotiating a salary across borders, always compare net figures — gross means very different things depending on where you work.

title: Rethinking AI API Costs: It's About the Workflow
title_hi: एआई एपीआई लागत: यह केवल कॉल नहीं, पूरी प्रक्रिया है
summary: Rethink AI API costs: focus on workflow, not model calls
content:
The cost of AI APIs is often misunderstood. While cost per model call is a factor, it's not the primary economic unit of an agentic product. One user action can trigger a chain of events, including retrieval, planner calls, tool calls, and validation, resulting in multiple paid decisions. This can lead to surprise margins for teams building AI products. The key is to consider the customer outcome cost across the entire workflow, rather than just the model call cost. For instance, 'answering a support question' may involve multiple steps, including retrieval, classification, and routing. 
   The traditional question of 'how much does this model cost per million tokens?' is no longer sufficient. Instead, teams should ask 'what does this customer outcome cost across the whole workflow?' This shift in perspective can help teams better understand their AI API costs and make more informed decisions. 
   By considering the entire workflow, teams can optimize their AI API usage and reduce costs. This requires a deeper understanding of the complex interactions between different components of the AI system and how they contribute to the overall cost.
content_hi:
एआई एपीआई लागत अक्सर गलत तरीके से समझी जाती है। मॉडल कॉल की लागत एक कारक है, लेकिन यह एक एजेंटिक उत्पाद की प्राथमिक आर्थिक इकाई नहीं है। एक उपयोगकर्ता की क्रिया एक श्रृंखला को ट्रिगर कर सकती है, जिसमें पुनर्प्राप्ति, योजनाकार कॉल, टूल कॉल और सत्यापन शामिल हैं, जिससे कई भुगतान निर्णय होते हैं। 
   यह टीमों के लिए आश्चर्यजनक मार्जिन का कारण बन सकता है जो एआई उत्पादों का निर्माण कर रहे हैं। मुख्य बात यह है कि पूरी प्रक्रिया में ग्राहक परिणाम लागत पर विचार करना, न कि केवल मॉडल कॉल लागत पर। 
   उदाहरण के लिए, 'एक समर्थन प्रश्न का उत्तर देना' कई चरणों को शामिल कर सकता है, जिसमें पुनर्प्राप्ति, वर्गीकरण और मार्गदर्शन शामिल हैं। पारंपरिक प्रश्न 'यह मॉडल एक मिलियन टोकन प्रति कितना खर्च करता है?' अब पर्याप्त नहीं है। इसके बजाय, टीमों को 'पूरी प्रक्रिया में यह ग्राहक परिणाम कितना खर्च करता है?' पूछना चाहिए।

title: Unlocking SQL Magic: AI-Powered Text-to-SQL Solutions

summary: AI-powered Text-to-SQL solutions for efficient database querying
content: As data grows exponentially, accessing and querying databases efficiently becomes a crucial bottleneck. Artificial Intelligence can bridge this gap by translating human language into executable SQL queries. Text-to-SQL systems work by understanding the schema of a database and the intent of a user's question, allowing users to ask questions in natural language instead of writing complex SQL queries. For instance, a user can ask, 'What was the total revenue for electronics this year?' and the AI model will generate the corresponding SQL query.

title: Halo Evolves: Multi-Agent System Unveiled
title_hi: हेलो का नवाचार: बहु-एजेंट प्रणाली का पदार्पण
summary: Halo's multi-agent system revolution
content: Halo is shifting from a single-agent to a multi-agent system, dividing its responsibilities into five distinct agents. This change aims to enhance the pentesting process by assigning separate tasks to each agent, such as planning, execution, and correction. The current single-agent system has limitations, including crowded context and muddier reasoning over time. By splitting these tasks, each agent will have a smaller, more focused job and a dedicated context window, leading to more efficient and effective pentesting. This new approach will enable Halo to integrate more tools and improve its overall performance.

title: 5 Key Decisions I'd Make Differently in My Chrome Extension
title_hi: मैंने एक क्रोम एक्सटेंशन बनाया जो पीआर समीक्षा के लिए दैनिक उपयोग किया जाता है — 5 निर्णय जो मैं अलग तरह से करूंगा
summary: Learn from my mistakes in building a Chrome extension for PR review
content: As a developer, I've spent most of my career reviewing pull requests. To streamline this process, I built PR Focus Pro, a Chrome extension that uses AI to triage GitHub PRs. The extension has been in production for 6 weeks and I use it daily. One of the key challenges I faced was storing state in module-level variables, which was silently killed by the MV3 service worker. If I were to start again, I'd reconsider this approach. The background script would need a more robust solution to handle state management.
content_hi: मैंने अपने करियर के अधिकांश समय पुल अनुरोधों की समीक्षा की है। इस प्रक्रिया को सुव्यवस्थित करने के लिए, मैंने पीआर फोकस प्रो बनाया, एक क्रोम एक्सटेंशन जो जीआईटीहब पीआर को एआई के साथ वर्गीकृत करता है। यह एक्सटेंशन 6 सप्ताह से उत्पादन में है और मैं इसका दैनिक उपयोग करता हूं। मुझे जिन चुनौतियों का सामना करना पड़ा, उनमें से एक मॉड्यूल-स्तरीय वेरिएबल्स में स्थिति संग्रहीत करना था, जिसे एमवी3 सेवा कार्यकर्ता द्वारा चुपचाप मार दिया गया था।

title: 5 Lessons Learned from Building a Chrome Extension for PR Review
title_hi: पीआर समीक्षा के लिए क्रोम एक्सटेंशन बनाने से सीखे गए 5 सबक
summary: Chrome extension for PR review: 5 lessons learned
content: As a developer, I've spent most of my career reviewing pull requests. The queue became overwhelming, prompting me to ask why this kept happening. The answer was the lack of a system, just a flat list of open PRs and a vague sense of urgency. I built PR Focus Pro, a Chrome extension that triages GitHub PRs with AI summaries and a hybrid risk score. In hindsight, there are several decisions I'd make differently, including storing state in module-level variables, which was silently killed by the MV3 service worker.
content_hi: एक डेवलपर के रूप में, मैंने अपने अधिकांश करियर में पुल अनुरोधों की समीक्षा की है। कतार इतनी खराब हो गई कि मैंने पूछना बंद कर दिया कि 'मुझे पहले कौन सा पीआर समीक्षा करना चाहिए?' और 'यह क्यों होता रहता है?' पूछना शुरू कर दिया। इसका उत्तर यह था कि मेरे पास कोई प्रणाली नहीं थी, सिर्फ एक खुले पीआर की एक समतल सूची और एक अस्पष्ट उत्साह की भावना। मैंने पीआर फोकस प्रो बनाया, एक क्रोम एक्सटेंशन जो एआई सारांश और एक हाइब्रिड जोखिम स्कोर के साथ गिटहब पीआर को ट्राइज करता है।

title: 5 Mistakes to Avoid When Building an AI Chrome Extension
title_hi: एआई क्रोम एक्सटेंशन बनाते समय 5 गलतियाँ
summary: Avoid common mistakes when building AI Chrome extensions
content: As a seasoned developer, I've spent most of my career reviewing pull requests. However, the queue became unmanageable, prompting me to build PR Focus Pro, a Chrome extension that utilizes AI summaries and a hybrid risk score to triage GitHub PRs. After six weeks in production, I've identified five key mistakes made during its development. One major error was storing state in module-level variables, which were silently killed by the MV3 service worker. This resulted in a blank screen for users, leading them to believe the extension was broken.
content_hi: एक अनुभवी डेवलपर के रूप में, मैंने अपने अधिकांश करियर पुल रिक्वेस्ट की समीक्षा करने में बिताया है। हालाँकि, कतार असंभव हो गई, जिसने मुझे पीआर फोकस प्रो बनाने के लिए प्रेरित किया, एक क्रोम एक्सटेंशन जो गिटहब पुल रिक्वेस्ट को त्रिभुज करने के लिए एआई सारांश और एक हाइब्रिड जोखिम स्कोर का उपयोग करता है। उत्पादन में छह सप्ताह के बाद, मैंने इसके विकास के दौरान की गई पाँच प्रमुख गलतियों की पहचान की है। एक बड़ी गलती मॉड्यूल-लेवल वेरिएबल्स में स्थिति संग्रहीत करना थी, जो एमवी3 सेवा कार्यकर्ता द्वारा चुपचाप मारे गए थे।

title: Preventing Stale Fetch Requests
title_hi: पुराने फेच अनुरोधों को रोकना
summary: Fix stale fetch requests
content: When a component triggers a fetch() and then unmounts, the request continues to run. This can lead to stale closures, dead state setters, and overwritten UI results. A common example is the search input, where multiple requests can be in flight at the same time, causing incorrect results to be displayed. To fix this, we can use the AbortController API to cancel previous requests when a new one starts.
content_hi: जब एक घटक fetch() को ट्रिगर करता है और फिर अनमाउंट हो जाता है, तो अनुरोध चलना जारी रखता है। इससे पुराने क्लोजर, मृत राज्य सेटर और ओवरराइटन यूआई परिणाम हो सकते हैं। एक सामान्य उदाहरण खोज इनपुट है, जहां एक ही समय में कई अनुरोध उड़ान में हो सकते हैं, जिससे गलत परिणाम प्रदर्शित होते हैं। इसे ठीक करने के लिए, हम AbortController एपीआई का उपयोग कर सकते हैं ताकि जब एक नया अनुरोध शुरू हो तो पिछले अनुरोधों को रद्द किया जा सके।

title: I Open-Sourced My Business: The Cold Hard Truth
title_hi: मैंने अपना व्यवसाय ओपन-सोर्स कर दिया: ठंडी सच्चाई
summary: Open-sourcing my business: why it's a strategic move
content: As a solo founder, I've open-sourced my product under MIT, including the detector, frozen parameters, and evaluation harness. This may seem counterintuitive, but my bottleneck is accessing real system traces, which are sensitive data. By making my code open, I can assure potential users that their data never has to leave their machine. This openness is not about generosity, but about building trust. My competitive advantage lies not in the code itself, but in my public track record of transparency and honesty. Open-sourcing my code makes my claims falsifiable, allowing others to verify my results.
content_hi: मैंने अपने उत्पाद को एमआईटी लाइसेंस के तहत ओपन-सोर्स कर दिया है, जिसमें डिटेक्टर, फ्रोजन पैरामीटर और मूल्यांकन हार्नेस शामिल हैं। यह अनोखा लगता है, लेकिन मेरी बोतलनेक वास्तविक प्रणाली ट्रेसेस तक पहुंच है, जो संवेदनशील डेटा हैं। अपने कोड को ओपन करके, मैं संभावित उपयोगकर्ताओं को आश्वस्त कर सकता हूं कि उनका डेटा कभी भी उनकी मशीन से बाहर नहीं जाता है। यह खुलापन दान नहीं है, बल्कि विश्वास बनाने के बारे में है। मेरा प्रतिस्पर्धी लाभ कोड में नहीं है, बल्कि मेरे सार्वजनिक रिकॉर्ड में पारदर्शिता और ईमानदारी है।

title: Week 19: Rethinking Student Needs
title_hi: सप्ताह 19: छात्रों की जरूरतों पर पुनर्विचार
summary: Rethinking student needs, beyond answers
content: When building BAINT, we thought students needed answers. However, after speaking with many students, we realized the problem goes deeper. A Mass Communication student admitted that Data Analysis in Communication Research was their most challenging course. Upon asking why, the student simply replied 'Everything', highlighting the overwhelming nature of the learning experience. This conversation changed our approach, focusing on helping students understand the information they already have, rather than providing more notes or explanations.
content_hi: बैंट बनाते समय, हमें लगा कि छात्रों को उत्तर चाहिए। लेकिन कई छात्रों से बात करने के बाद, हमें एहसास हुआ कि समस्या गहरी है। एक मास कम्युनिकेशन छात्र ने स्वीकार किया कि डेटा विश्लेषण उनके लिए सबसे कठिन विषय था। जब हमने पूछा क्यों तो छात्र ने बस 'सब कुछ' कहा, जिससे सीखने के अनुभव की भारी प्रकृति को उजागर किया गया।

title: Designing a Secure AI Receptionist Workflow for SMEs in Argentina
title_hi: अर्जेंटीना में एसएमई के लिए सुरक्षित एआई रिसेप्शनिस्ट कार्यप्रवाह का डिज़ाइन
summary: AI receptionist workflow design for SMEs in Argentina
content: A useful AI receptionist resembles a telephone intake system with explicit limits. The minimal architecture should separate intention, business rules, and handoff. The system should identify the caller's intent, such as scheduling a turn or making a reservation, and provide a summary for the team to take action. Guardrails are essential before production, including not inventing prices or promises, and deriving sensitive situations. The system should be tested with various scenarios, such as normal consultations, price inquiries, and complaints.
content_hi: एक उपयोगी एआई रिसेप्शनिस्ट एक टेलीफोन इंटेक सिस्टम की तरह है जिसमें स्पष्ट सीमाएं हैं। न्यूनतम आर्किटेक्चर को तीन चीजों को अलग करना चाहिए: इरादा, व्यवसाय नियम, और हैंडओवर। सिस्टम को कॉलर के इरादे की पहचान करनी चाहिए, जैसे कि एक मोड़ या आरक्षण करना, और टीम के लिए एक सारांश प्रदान करना चाहिए। उत्पादन से पहले गार्डरेल आवश्यक है, जिसमें कीमतें या वादे नहीं बनाना और संवेदनशील स्थितियों को व्युत्पन्न करना शामिल है।

title: Commodore's 'Dumbphone' Takes the World by Storm
title_hi: कमोडोर का 'डम्बफोन' बना सबकी पहली पसंद
summary: Commodore's new 'dumbphone' is a hit
content: In a surprising turn of events, Commodore's latest 'dumbphone' has taken the market by storm, with the company claiming it's been their biggest week yet. The phone, which boasts a privacy-first approach and lacks social media, seems to have struck a chord with those embracing digital minimalism. content_hi: एक आश्चर्यजनक मोड़ में, कमोडोर का नवीनतम 'डम्बफोन' बाजार में तूफान ले आया है, कंपनी ने दावा किया है कि यह उनका सबसे बड़ा सप्ताह रहा है। यह फोन, जो गोपनीयता-प्रथम दृष्टिकोण का दावा करता है और सोशल मीडिया की कमी है, जैसे कि डिजिटल न्यूनतमवाद को अपनाने वालों के साथ तालमेल बिठाने के लिए लगता है। दिलचस्प बात यह है, इसके 'डम्बफोन' नाम के बावजूद, डिवाइस अभी भी व्हाट्सएप को सपोर्ट करता है, जिससे उपयोगकर्ता अन्य सोशल मीडिया प्लेटफ़ॉर्म की विकर्षणों के बिना जुड़े रह सकते हैं।

title: Building a Secure Login Page with React
title_hi: रीऐक्ट में पुन: प्रयोज्य घटकों के साथ लॉगिन पृष्ठ का निर्माण
summary: Learn to build a login page with React
content: To create a login page for the Smart Budget Tracker app, we'll utilize reusable components discussed in our previous post. This component requires two text inputs for email/username and password, a login button, and API calls for authentication and authorization. We define the LoginForm interface with email and password fields, and set up the required states for the form, including loginForm, loading, and error. The login page is then built using reusable components, including a sidebar and a main section with a welcome message.
content_hi: स्मार्ट बजट ट्रैकर ऐप के लिए एक लॉगिन पृष्ठ बनाने के लिए, हम अपने पिछले पोस्ट में चर्चा किए गए पुन: प्रयोज्य घटकों का उपयोग करेंगे। इस घटक को ईमेल/उपयोगकर्ता नाम और पासवर्ड के लिए दो टेक्स्ट इनपुट, एक लॉगिन बटन, और प्रमाणीकरण अनुरोध भेजने और प्राधिकरण प्रतिक्रिया प्राप्त करने के लिए एपीआई कॉल की आवश्यकता होती है। हम लॉगिनफॉर्म इंटरफ़ेस को ईमेल और पासवर्ड फील्ड के साथ परिभाषित करते हैं और फॉर्म के लिए आवश्यक स्टेट सेट करते हैं।

title: Download All AWS S3 Bucket Folders with a Single Script
title_hi: एकल स्क्रिप्ट के साथ सभी एएवीएस एस3 बकेट फ़ोल्डर्स डाउनलोड करें
summary: Download all AWS S3 bucket folders with a single script
content: Today, I needed to download all folders from my AWS S3 bucket. However, the button to do so was disabled. To overcome this, I created a script that downloads all folders, including images, videos, and fonts. This script utilizes the AWS SDK to interact with the S3 bucket and the 'fs' and 'path' modules to handle file system operations. The script is designed to be efficient and easy to use, making it a valuable tool for projects that require downloading large numbers of files from S3 buckets.
content_hi: आज, मुझे अपने एएवीएस एस3 बकेट से सभी फ़ोल्डर्स डाउनलोड करने की आवश्यकता थी। हालांकि, ऐसा करने के लिए बटन अक्षम था। इसे पार करने के लिए, मैंने एक स्क्रिप्ट बनाई जो सभी फ़ोल्डर्स, जिनमें छवियां, वीडियो और फ़ॉन्ट शामिल हैं, डाउनलोड करती है। यह स्क्रिप्ट एस3 बकेट के साथ बातचीत करने के लिए एएवीएस एसडीके का उपयोग करती है और फ़ाइल सिस्टम संचालन को संभालने के लिए 'fs' और 'path' मॉड्यूल का उपयोग करती है।

title: I Asked 3 AI Coding Tools to Fix the Same Bug. The Results Were Shocking.
title_hi: मैंने 3 एआई कोडिंग टूल्स से एक ही बग को ठीक करने के लिए कहा। परिणाम चौंकाने वाले थे।

title: Shopify API Version 2025-07 Retirement: What You Need to Know
title_hi: शोपिफाई एपीआई संस्करण 2025-07 सेवानिवृत्ति: आपको क्या जानने की आवश्यकता है
summary: Shopify API version 2025-07 retirement: silent fall forward to 2025-10
content: On July 16, 2026, Shopify will retire API version 2025-07. If your app still uses this version, don't expect errors - requests will silently fall forward to 2025-10. This can cause issues with deprecated fields, changed types, and altered enum values. The change is a breaking one, but without loud errors, making it a dangerous kind of deprecation. Developers must test their apps against the new version to avoid issues.
content_hi: 16 जुलाई, 2026 को शोपिफाई एपीआई संस्करण 2025-07 को सेवानिवृत्त कर देगा। यदि आपका ऐप अभी भी इस संस्करण का उपयोग करता है, तो त्रुटियों की अपेक्षा न करें - अनुरोध चुपचाप 2025-10 में गिर जाएंगे। यह पुराने क्षेत्रों, परिवर्तित प्रकारों और परिवर्तित एनम मानों के साथ समस्याएं पैदा कर सकता है। यह परिवर्तन एक विफलकारी है, लेकिन तेज त्रुटियों के बिना, जिससे यह एक खतरनाक प्रकार की अवसान है। डेवलपर्स को अपने ऐप्स को नई संस्करण के साथ परीक्षण करने की आवश्यकता है ताकि समस्याओं से बचा जा सके।

title: Time Tracking in Mid-Sized Businesses: More Than Just Compliance
title_hi: मध्यम आकार के व्यवसायों में डिजिटल समय ट्रैकिंग: केवल अनुपालन से अधिक
summary: Discover how digital time tracking can transform your mid-sized business
content:
As a mid-sized business owner, managing time tracking can be a daunting task. However, digital time tracking is more than just a legal requirement. It can increase efficiency, reduce costs, and provide valuable insights. By implementing a digital time tracking system, businesses can simplify the process of tracking employee work hours, analyze project times, and boost productivity. This not only helps with compliance but also provides a competitive edge. With the right solution, businesses can streamline their operations and make data-driven decisions. 
   The benefits of digital time tracking are numerous, including increased accuracy, reduced administrative burden, and improved employee satisfaction. By automating time tracking, businesses can free up resources and focus on growth and development. 
   In conclusion, digital time tracking is a game-changer for mid-sized businesses, offering a range of benefits that go beyond mere compliance.
content_hi:
एक मध्यम आकार के व्यवसाय के मालिक के रूप में, समय ट्रैकिंग का प्रबंधन एक चुनौतीपूर्ण काम हो सकता है। हालांकि, डिजिटल समय ट्रैकिंग केवल एक कानूनी आवश्यकता से अधिक है। यह दक्षता बढ़ा सकता है, लागत कम कर सकता है, और मूल्यवान अंतर्दृष्टि प्रदान कर सकता है। एक डिजिटल समय ट्रैकिंग प्रणाली को लागू करके, व्यवसाय कर्मचारी कार्य घंटों को ट्रैक करने की प्रक्रिया को सरल बना सकते हैं, परियोजना समय का विश्लेषण कर सकते हैं, और उत्पादकता बढ़ा सकते हैं। 
   डिजिटल समय ट्रैकिंग के लाभ कई हैं, जिनमें सटीकता में वृद्धि, प्रशासनिक बोझ में कमी, और कर्मचारी संतुष्टि में सुधार शामिल हैं। समय ट्रैकिंग को स्वचालित करके, व्यवसाय संसाधनों को मुक्त कर सकते हैं और विकास पर ध्यान केंद्रित कर सकते हैं।
   निष्कर्ष में, मध्यम आकार के व्यवसायों के लिए डिजिटल समय ट्रैकिंग एक खेल-परिवर्तक है, जो केवल अनुपालन से परे कई लाभ प्रदान करता है।

title: AI-Powered Cover Letter Generator
title_hi: नौकरी के विवरण की नकल करने वाला कवर लेटर जनरेटर
summary: AI cover letter generator, tailored to job postings
content: Most cover letter tools provide a generic template, requiring manual effort to tailor it to the job description. I created an AI-powered cover letter generator that mirrors the actual job posting, increasing the chances of passing through applicant tracking systems and impressing human recruiters. The tool takes the job description and a brief background, generating a specific, ATS-ready letter that reflects the role's requirements. This innovation streamlines the application process, making it easier to create tailored cover letters with minimal effort. The generator uses Cloudflare Pages with Functions for hosting and Claude for letter generation, ensuring fast and reliable performance.
content_hi: अधिकांश कवर लेटर टूल एक सामान्य टेम्पलेट प्रदान करते हैं, जिसमें नौकरी विवरण के अनुसार इसे अनुकूलित करने के लिए मैनुअल प्रयास की आवश्यकता होती है। मैंने एक एआई-संचालित कवर लेटर जनरेटर बनाया है जो वास्तविक नौकरी पोस्टिंग की नकल करता है, जिससे आवेदक ट्रैकिंग सिस्टम और मानव भर्तीकर्ता दोनों को प्रभावित करने की संभावना बढ़ जाती है। यह टूल नौकरी विवरण और एक संक्षिप्त पृष्ठभूमि लेता है, एक विशिष्ट, एटीएस-तैयार पत्र तैयार करता है जो भूमिका की आवश्यकताओं को प्रतिबिंबित करता है।

title: Merging Memories: A Tool to Reunite Families in Photos
title_hi: स्मृतियों को मिलाना: एक परिवार को फोटो में पुनः मिलने का उपकरण
summary: Reunite with loved ones in photos
content: Some family photos have a person-shaped gap in them. A grandparent who passed before the reunion or a family member deployed or living overseas who couldn't make the shot. Memorial Merge gently adds that person into the group photo as one natural picture, with matched light and tones. title: Choosing the Right Backend Framework: Django vs. Gin vs. Ruby on Rails.
title_hi: Choosing the Right Backend Framework: Django vs. Gin vs. Ruby on Rails.
summary: Every application we use today—from banking apps to social media platforms—has something working behind the scenes. That
content:
Every application we use today—from banking apps to social media platforms—has something working behind the scenes. That hidden engine is called the backend.
The backend is responsible for processing requests, storing data, handling authentication, enforcing business rules, and ensuring everything works as expected when users interact with an application.
One of the first decisions backend developers make is choosing a framework. A framework provides the tools, structure, and best practices needed to build applications faster and more securely.
Today, let's look at three popular backend frameworks: Django, Gin, and Ruby on Rails.


Django (Python)
Django is one of the most mature and feature-rich backend frameworks available. Built using Python, it follows the philosophy of "batteries included."
This means many features developers need are already built into the framework, including:
User authentication
Admin dashboard
Database ORM
Security protections
URL routing
Form validation
Because so much comes ready to use, developers can spend more time solving business problems instead of rebuilding common features.
Best for:
Content management systems
E-learning platforms
Business applications
APIs
Startups building products quickly
Advantages:
Fast development
Excellent security features
Large community
Extensive documentation
Scales well for many applications
Trade-offs:
The framework includes many components, so it can feel heavier than minimalist frameworks.
Gin (Go)
Gin is a lightweight web framework built for the Go programming language.
Unlike Django, Gin keeps things minimal. It gives developers speed and flexibility while letting them choose many of the additional tools they want to use.
One reason many developers enjoy Gin is its impressive performance. Since Go is a compiled language designed for concurrency, Gin can efficiently handle many requests simultaneously while using relatively few system resources.
Best for:
REST APIs
Microservices
High-performance systems
Real-time services
Cloud-native applications
Advantages:
Extremely fast
Lightweight
Excellent concurrency support
Easy to deploy
Great for scalable services
Trade-offs:
Developers often need to choose and integrate additional libraries themselves, which can mean more setup than frameworks like Django.
Ruby on Rails (Ruby)
Ruby on Rails has influenced modern web development more than many people realize.
It introduced ideas like Convention over Configuration, allowing developers to spend less time configuring projects and more time building features.
Rails emphasizes developer productivity. Many startups have used it to build products quickly because it helps developers move from idea to working application in a short time.
Best for:
Startup MVPs
SaaS applications
E-commerce platforms
Social platforms
Business applications
Advantages:
Rapid development
Clean project structure
Large ecosystem
Mature tooling
Excellent developer experience
Trade-offs:
Rails may not match Go-based frameworks in raw performance, though it is more than capable for many production applications.
Which One Should You Learn?
There isn't a single "best" framework. The right choice depends on the kind of problems you're solving.
Choose Django if:
You enjoy Python.
You want lots of built-in functionality.
You want to build complete applications quickly.
Choose Gin if:
You enjoy Go.
Performance matters.
You're interested in cloud-native development and microservices.
Choose Ruby on Rails if:
You value developer productivity.
You want to build products rapidly.
You appreciate clear conventions and an opinionated framework.
Final Thoughts
A framework is just a tool. What matters most is understanding backend fundamentals—HTTP, databases, authentication, APIs, security, caching, and application architecture. Once you understand these concepts, learning a new framework becomes much easier.
Rather than chasing whichever framework is trending today, invest in mastering the principles behind backend development. Frameworks evolve, but solid engineering fundamentals remain valuable throughout your career.
As I continue learning backend development, one thing has become increasingly clear: every framework has its strengths. title: The SaaS Affiliate Strategy That Pays Monthly (Not Just Once)
title_hi: The SaaS Affiliate Strategy That Pays Monthly (Not Just Once)
summary:
Look, i almost walked past this opportunity three times. And I'm kicking myself for it.
For two years I ran a half-decen
content:
Look, i almost walked past this opportunity three times. And I'm kicking myself for it.
For two years I ran a half-decent affiliate side hustle promoting one-off software products. You know the type — landing page builders, email tools, hosting providers. The commissions looked juicy on paper ($200 per signup, $500 per sale), but here's the problem I kept staring at in my Notion tracker: every single dollar was one-time. I had to keep finding new traffic, keep running new ads, keep publishing new content just to keep the income flat month over month.
Then I discovered SaaS affiliate programs with a recurring commission structure, and the math completely changed.
Let me break this down the way I'd explain it to a coworker at my day job as a backend dev.


  
  
  Why Recurring Commissions Are a Different Animal


Here's the math on why one-time versus recurring is night and day, using actual numbers from my tracker.
In 2024, I made roughly $11,400 from one-off affiliate promotions. Sounds decent, right? But the work never stopped. I logged 412 hours on that hustle across the year — that's content creation, ad spend management, customer DMs, and refund disputes. That works out to about $27.67 per hour. Not bad, not great, definitely not life-changing.
Compare that to what I started doing in early 2025 with a recurring SaaS affiliate program. In eight months, I've pulled in $6,800 from a single program — and the income hasn't dropped off. The cumulative revenue is actually accelerating because every new signup keeps paying me month after month. Once I hit a critical mass of around 80-100 active referrals, the monthly payouts started covering my car payment. That's when I knew this was a real thing, not just another side hustle grind.
The compounding effect is what people miss. With one-time commissions, your income is a function of this month's effort. With recurring commissions, your income is a function of all your past effort combined.


  
  
  The Program I'm Using (And Why)


I'm not going to bury the lede — the program that's been paying me monthly is Global API's affiliate program. They offer 15% on first orders and 8% recurring commission on every renewal after that.
Let me run the numbers on why that structure matters.
Say I refer a customer who signs up for a $99/month plan. Month one, I earn 15% of $99 = $14.85. Then every single month after that, as long as they stay subscribed, I earn 8% of $99 = $7.92. That's $7.92 per month, per customer, that I don't have to lift a finger for after the initial referral.
If that customer stays for 12 months, I've earned $14.85 + (11 × $7.92) = $101.97 from a single signup. If they stay for 24 months, I'm at $189.09. That's nearly double the original first-order commission.
My current customer retention is around 73% at the six-month mark, based on what I can see in my dashboard. That means roughly three out of four people I refer keep paying month after month. That's a far better retention rate than I expected, and it makes the recurring math work beautifully.
There's also a premium tier offering 10% commission for higher-volume partners, which I'll touch on later.


  
  
  What Exactly Am I Promoting?


Here's where I have to be careful about how I describe what Global API does, because I don't want to make this sound like a generic AI comparison article. What matters for my affiliate business is this: Global API gives users access to 150+ AI models through a single API key.
Why does that matter for affiliate marketers? Because I don't have to be an AI expert to sell this. I just need to find people who already know they need AI API access and are frustrated with juggling multiple provider accounts, billing relationships, and integrations.
My target customer is a developer or small agency owner who wants to add AI features to their product without signing up for five different services. That's a growing audience, and they're actively searching for solutions.


  
  
  How I Built My Funnel (Without Burning Out)


I work a 9-to-6 job as a backend engineer. I have maybe 8-12 hours per week to put into side projects. So I needed a strategy that didn't require me to become a full-time content creator.
Here's what's worked:
A focused blog with about 20 posts — I write one or two articles per month targeting long-tail keywords that my ideal customers actually search for. I'm not trying to rank for competitive head terms. I'm going after things like "AI API for [specific use case]" or "alternative to [specific competitor]" patterns.
A simple landing page — I built a single comparison and review page that walks visitors through what Global API offers, who it's for, and how to get started. It converts at around 4.2%, which is way higher than any of my one-time affiliate pages ever did.
A small email list of about 600 developers — I send a monthly email with one or two practical tips for working with AI APIs. The list has been growing organically through my content. When I mention Global API, it converts at about 2.8%.
The total time investment per month? Roughly 10-12 hours. That's content writing, email drafting, and the occasional customer question reply. Per hour, that puts me at a much higher effective rate than anything I did before.


  
  
  My Income Breakdown (The Real Numbers)


I'm a numbers guy, so let me show you exactly what my Notion tracker looks like for the past eight months.


Month 1: $94 (first trickle of referrals starting to convert)
Month 2: $312
Month 3: $587
Month 4: $891
Month 5: $1,124
Month 6: $1,243
Month 7: $1,289
Month 8: $1,260
Total: $6,800 over eight months. Average: $850/month. Trend: still slowly climbing as my recurring base grows.
The "trick" is that Month 8 income came from roughly 40% effort and 60% residual. Once you have enough subscribers, the recurring tail does most of the work.
Here's a per-hour calculation I ran recently: in Month 8, I spent about 9 hours on the business. That's $140 per hour. Compare that to my day job's effective hourly rate after taxes and commute time, and this side hustle is actually out-earning my main job on a per-hour basis.
I should note — those numbers are a mix of the 15% first-order and 8% recurring payouts. I haven't yet hit the volume threshold for the 10% premium tier, but it's on my radar for later this year.
#
# What I'd Do Differently If I Started Today
If I were starting over from scratch, I'd skip the first two months of fumbling and go straight to what worked.
First, I'd build the landing page on day one. Don't wait until you have content ranked. The landing page is your conversion engine, and everything else feeds into it.
Second, I'd focus on one traffic channel. I wasted time trying to do YouTube, Twitter, and SEO simultaneously. SEO won for me because it compounds — every article I publish still drives traffic six months later. Tweets are gone in 24 hours. Pick the channel that matches your skills and stick with it.
Third, I'd start tracking everything from day one. My Notion tracker has columns for: referral source, plan tier, signup date, monthly recurring revenue per customer, churn date (if applicable), and cumulative commission earned. That data is what lets me make decisions instead of guessing.
#
# The Math on Scale
Let me sketch out what the next 12 months could look like if I maintain my current pace.
If my referral base grows from the current ~95 active customers to around 200 by next August, and my average customer is paying about $85/month for their plan, here's the projection:
200 customers × $85/month average plan value × 8% recurring = $1,360/month in pure recurring income
Plus ongoing first-order commissions from new referrals, probably another $400-600/month in months where I'm actively marketing
That puts my realistic 12-month target at roughly $2,000/month in recurring revenue, with maybe $1,500/month in first-order bonuses on top during active months. Total annual income from this single program: $25,000-30,000.
For a side hustle that takes 10 hours a week, that's a meaningful number. And the kicker is — once I hit that $2,000/month recurring baseline, my annual income from this program alone would be $24,000 even if I stopped all marketing activity and let churn do its thing.
That's the power of recurring. It's an asset, not a transaction.
#
# Who This Works Best For
I want to be honest about who should and shouldn't pursue this.
This works great if you:
Already have some kind of audience (even a small one — 200 email subscribers is enough to start)
Are comfortable writing or creating educational content about developer tools
Can commit 8-15 hours per week for at least 6 months before judging results
Like the idea of building an asset that pays you monthly rather than chasing one-off sales
This won't work for you if you:
Need money in the next 30 days
Hate writing or content creation
Want completely passive income with zero upfront work (spoiler: that doesn't exist)
Don't have any audience or traffic source
#
# My Day Job + Side Hustle Balance
For what it's worth, my employer doesn't know about the affiliate income. There's nothing in my contract that prohibits it, but I keep the two worlds separate. I don't promote anything during work hours. I don't use company resources. I run everything out of my personal Notion workspace and a separate bank account.
That's probably overkill, but it keeps things clean. If you have a strict non-compete or moonlighting policy, read it carefully before you start.
#
# Where to Go From Here
If you've made it this far and you're the kind of person who tracks everything in a spreadsheet and thinks in terms of ROI, you already know whether this opportunity fits your situation. The question is whether you're going to do anything about it.
I'm not going to pretend this is some magic money button. It's work. But it's the first side hustle I've run where the income actually feels like it's compounding rather than resetting every month.
The program that's been paying me is Global API's affiliate program — 15% on first orders, 8% recurring on renewals, with a premium tier offering 10% for higher-volume partners. If you have any kind of audience in the developer, SaaS, or AI tooling space, it's worth a serious look.
You can check out the program details and sign up at https://global-apis.com/affiliate. I've been with them for eight months, payouts have been on time every single month, and the support team actually responds when I have questions. That alone puts them ahead of half the affiliate programs I've tried.
Run the math on your own audience. Build a landing page. Track your numbers. Give it six months. If it works, you'll have a recurring income stream that keeps paying you long after you stop working on it. That's the whole game.

summary: EU AI Act compliance for AI systems
content: The EU AI Act, which came into force on August 1, 2024, is a significant regulatory framework for AI systems. It classifies AI systems into four risk tiers: Prohibited, High Risk, Limited Risk, and Minimal Risk. Our RAG system falls under the Limited Risk category, requiring us to disclose to users that they are interacting with AI. To ensure compliance, we need to establish a governance structure, conduct risk assessments, and maintain audit logs.

title: I Almost Gave Up... But One Console Log Changed Everything
title_hi: I Almost Gave Up... But One Console Log Changed Everything
summary:
Today I spent hours chasing a bug that looked impossible.

When I clicked "Add Cabin", nothing happened.

No modal.

No
content:
Today I spent hours chasing a bug that looked impossible.

When I clicked "Add Cabin", nothing happened.

No modal.

No error.

Nothing.

At first, I thought the problem was in my React state.

Then I thought it was styled-components.

Then I suspected PropTypes.

I kept changing code, but the bug was still there.

Instead of randomly editing files, I decided to debug step by step.


  
  
  Step 1: Check if the button works


I added a simple log:



console.log("Button clicked");






The console printed:



Button clicked






Great!

That meant the click event was working.





  
  
  Step 2: Check the modal state


Next, I logged my modal state.



console.log({ openName, name });






I expected to see:



openName: "cabin-form"
name: "cabin-form"






Instead, I noticed something strange.

The value became "cabin-form" for only a moment, then immediately changed back to an empty string.

That was the biggest clue.





  
  
  Step 3: Ask the right question


Instead of asking,


"Why doesn't the modal open?"


I started asking,


"Who is closing the modal immediately?"


That changed everything.





  
  
  Step 4: Find the real culprit


The problem wasn't React.

It wasn't the modal.

It wasn't styled-components.

It was my outside click handler.

I had this:



document.addEventListener("click", handleClickOutside);






The same click that opened the modal was also triggering the outside click listener, so the modal closed instantly.





  
  
  Step 5: The fix


I changed it to:



document.addEventListener("mousedown", handleClickOutside);






And suddenly...

The modal opened perfectly.





  
  
  What I learned


Today taught me a lesson I'll remember for a long time.

Debugging isn't about guessing.

It's about collecting evidence.

Every console.log() is a clue.

Every observation eliminates another possibility.

The goal isn't to write more code.

The goal is to understand what the code is actually doing.

Today I didn't just fix a modal.

I learned how experienced developers think when they debug.

And that lesson is worth much more than fixing a single bug.

AI-powered critical date alerts can help save thousands by ensuring timely notifications and automated reminders. The Three-Tier Alert Framework, which includes Fire Drill, Heads Up, and Watch List alerts, is a key principle in implementing an effective alert system. Using tools like Google Calendar and email templates, property managers can streamline their workflow and reduce the risk of missed deadlines. For instance, when a lease is approaching its expiration date, the system sends a Fire Drill alert to the property manager, prompting them to take immediate action. By leveraging AI-powered tools, property managers can extract lease data, populate three-tier calendars, set up automated email digests, and ultimately stay on top of critical dates.
content_hi: एकल व्यावसायिक संपत्ति प्रबंधक के रूप में, पट्टा नवीनीकरण और किराया वृद्धि जैसी महत्वपूर्ण तिथियों पर नज़र रखना राजस्व खोने से बचने के लिए बहुत महत्वपूर्ण है। एआई-संचालित महत्वपूर्ण तिथि अलर्ट सSolo व्यावसायिक संपत्ति प्रबंधकों के लिए हजारों डॉलर बचा सकते हैं। तीन-स्तरीय अलर्ट फ्रेमवर्क, जिसमें फायर ड्रिल, हेड्स अप और वॉच लिस्ट अलर्ट शामिल हैं, एक प्रभावी अलर्ट प्रणाली लागू करने में एक महत्वपूर्ण सिद्धांत है। गूगल कैलेंडर और ईमेल टेम्पलेट्स जैसे उपकरणों का उपयोग करके, संपत्ति प्रबंधक अपने कार्य प्रवाह को सुव्यवस्थित कर सकते हैं और समय सीमा समाप्त होने के जोखिम को कम कर सकते हैं।

title: Google Search Console Impressions but No Clicks: What It Really Means and How to Fix It
title_hi: गूगल सर्च कंसोल में इम्प्रेशन लेकिन कोई क्लिक नहीं: इसका क्या अर्थ है और इसे कैसे ठीक करें
summary: Improve Google Search Console clicks
content: If you've noticed your page is getting impressions but no clicks in Google Search Console, you're not alone. Impressions are a positive signal, indicating Google has indexed your page and believes it's relevant. However, a lack of clicks suggests users are choosing other results. Understanding impressions vs clicks is key. An impression is counted when your page appears in search results, while a click happens when someone visits your website.
content_hi: यदि आप गूगल सर्च कंसोल में अपने पेज पर इम्प्रेशन देख रहे हैं लेकिन कोई क्लिक नहीं हो रहा है, तो आप अकेले नहीं हैं। इम्प्रेशन एक सकारात्मक संकेत है, जो दर्शाता है कि गूगल ने आपके पेज को इंडेक्स किया है और इसे प्रासंगिक मानता है। लेकिन क्लिक की कमी से पता चलता है कि उपयोगकर्ता अन्य परिणाम चुन रहे हैं।
